subject: crs
text: Which of the following personalities presided over the Jewish trial of Jesus?
explanation: Pontius Pilate convicted Jesus of treason and declared that Jesus thought himself King of the Jews, and had Jesus crucified.

subject: crs
text: The miraculous crossing of the Red Sea by the Israelites shows __________

options.A: God's guidance and protection over His people
options.C: God's fulfilment of His promises to destroy the Egyptians.

explanation: After all the terrible frogs, flies, calamity, and death that God sent to the Egyptians, Pharaoh was ready for the Israelites to leave. The Egyptians even gave the Israelites silver, gold, jewelry, and clothing. The Egyptians gave them anything they asked for. They really wanted them to leave!

subject: crs
text: Which Persian King allowed the Jews to go back to rebuild the temple?
explanation: According to the Bible, Cyrus the Great, king of Persia, was the monarch under whom the Babylonian captivity ended. In the first year of his reign he was prompted by God to decree that the Temple in Jerusalem should be rebuilt and that such Jews as cared to might return to their land for this purpose.

subject: crs
text: In the temptation of Jesus, only Mark reported that Jesus was_____
explanation: Mark 1:13 = Mark says: "with beasts". "The Biblical meaning of temptation is 'a trial in which man has a free choice of being faithful or unfaithful to God'. Satan encouraged Jesus to deviate from the plan of his father by misusing his authority and privileges. Jesus used the Holy Scripture to resist all such temptation.

subject: crs
text: In the battle against Sisera, Deborah's co-operation with Barak was based on her

options.B: absolute belief and trust in God

explanation: Deborah realized that God would do much of the work through Barak so Barak was asked to lead the army.

subject: crs

explanation: Matthew was a dishonest tax collector driven by greed until Jesus Christ chose him as a disciple. It means salvation is for everybody how sinful we maybe

subject: crs
text: As Joseph revealed himself to his brothers, he told them that God allowed him to be sold into slavery so as to______________
explanation: The selling of Joseph was part of God's divine plan for him to save his tribes. He was placed in Egypt so as to preserve lives as a result of preserving food to avert the Famine

subject: crs
text: The parents of Moses did the following to ensure his survival except__________

options.C: Putting him in a padded basket

explanation: After Pharaoh had ordered all the first-born male babies to be killed, one woman, Jochebed, looked desperately for a way to save her newborn son. She hid Moses until he was three months old, she then put him in a basket made of reeds and left him on the river bed, knowing that Pharaoh's daughter came to bathe there. While he was left at the river bed, Moses's sister, Mariam, stood by and watch till pharaoh's daughter found the baby.

subject: crs
text: Which of the following was not an excuse Moses gave when he was called to deliver the Israelites?

options.A: The Israelites would not believe him

explanation: Moses did not use his past act of murder as an excuse when he was called to deliver the Israelites. The excuses he gave included doubts about the Israelites believing him (A), his slow speech (B), and his doubts about his own ability (D).

explanation: According to the second creation story in Genesis, beasts of the field and birds were formed out of the ground. This is described in Genesis 2:19 (RSV):

subject: crs
text: And the Lord said to Moses see, I make you as God to Pharaoh; and Aaron your bother - shall. which of the following roles was Aaron assigned?
explanation: In the passage you're referring to, Exodus 7:1, when the Lord says to Moses, "See, I make you as God to Pharaoh, and Aaron your brother shall be your prophet," Aaron is assigned the role of a prophet, not a priest. The term "prophet" here indicates that Aaron would speak on behalf of Moses to Pharaoh. Aaron later serves as a high priest in Israel, but his initial role is that of a prophet.

subject: crs
text: On the fifth day, the manifestation of the power of God's word was evident in the creation of the
explanation: According to Genesis, on the fifth day of creation, God said, "And God said, 'Let the waters bring forth swarms of living creatures, and let birds fly above the earth across the firmament of the heavens.'"(Genesis 1:20 - RSV).

subject: crs

options.B: freely of every tree except the ones with apples.
options.C: from the tree in the middle of the garden.
options.D: any animal and plant in Eden.

explanation: In Genesis, God's instruction to Adam regarding eating is presented as follows:

subject: crs
text: In which of the following ways did God evaluate His work after creation?
explanation: 31 And God saw everything that he had made, and behold, it was very good. And there was evening and there was morning, a sixth day - Genesis 1:31 (RSV)

subject: crs
text: What was the rationale behind Reuben placing Joseph into a dry well? To
explanation: The story can be found in Genesis 37:12-36

subject: crs
text: According to Pharaoh's dream, what did the seven gaunt and thin cows that came out of the Nile River do? They

options.C: ate all the seven ears of grains
options.D: ate up the seven sleek and fat cows

explanation: This dream is described in Genesis 41:1-7. In Pharaoh's dream, he saw seven fat and sleek cows emerging from the Nile River, followed by seven gaunt and thin cows. The thin cows then proceeded to devour or eat up the seven fat cows. This dream was interpreted by Joseph, who was known for interpreting dreams. He interpreted this particular dream as a prediction of seven years of abundance in Egypt followed by seven years of famine.

subject: crs
text: Which of the following was not a sign to convince the Israelites that Moses was sent by God?

options.C: River Nile becoming a pool of blood

explanation: The sign that is not specifically mentioned in the Bible as a convincing sign to the Israelites that Moses was sent by God is:

subject: crs
text: Which of the following creatures did God command into existence on the second day of creation?
explanation: B: Genesis 1:6-8: "And God said, Let there be a firmament in the midstof the waters, and let it divide the waters from the waters, And God made the firmament, and divided the waters which were under firmament from the waters which above the firmament: and it was so. And God called the firmament Heaven. And the evening and the morning were the second day."

subject: crs
text: The determination of times and seasons was made possible with the creation of ?
explanation: God created light for the determination of times and seasons. Genesis 1:14 -16. Genesis 1:14 - 19 provides the explanation; "and God said let there be light in the firmament of the heaven to divide the day from the night; and let them be for signs and for seasons and for days and years.

subject: crs
text: Which of the following was man's responsibility to other creatures?
explanation: B. Genesis 1: 26 - 28, "and God said, Let us make man in our image, after our likeness: and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the fowl of the air, and over the cattle, and over all the earth, and over every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth."

subject: crs
text: In which of the following ways did God prepare Joseph for his leadership role in Egypt? Through the

options.A: love his brothers had for him

explanation: B: God prepared Joseph for his leadership role in Egypt through the trials and tribulations he endured in his early life. Genesis 37-50 counts forJoseph's story, where he faces numerous challenges, including being sold into slavery by his jealous brothers, being falsely accused and imprisoned,and enduring years of separation from his family. Despite these hardships, Joseph remained faithful to God and continued to trust in His plan for his life. Through these trials Joseph developed important qualities such as resilience, humility, wisdom, and reliance on God. These experiences also provided him with valuable leadership skills, aqs he learnt to interpret dreams, manage resources,and govern effectively during his time in Egypt.

subject: crs
text: Which of the following was the greatest reason for which Joseph's brothers decided to do away with him?

options.B: his father made him a special robe
options.C: he was loved most by their father

explanation: D: The correct answer is the meaning of his dreams. Gemesis 37:3-8 provides insight into the reason why Josephs brothers haboured resentment towards him. Joseph's dreams further exacerbated the situation as they depicted him ruling over his brothers which angered them even more (Genesis 37:5-11). It was clearly stated in Genesis 37: 20 that Joseph's dreams of superiority contributed to their decision to plot against him and eventually sell him into slavery.

subject: crs
text: The twelve men Moses sent to spy out the land of Canaan were meant to verify the following except the

options.A: strength and weakness of the inhabitants
options.B: population and size of the land
options.C: size of the army and their weaponery
options.D: topography and fertility of the land

explanation: C: The correct answer to the question is size of the army and their weaponry. When Moses the twelve spies to explore the land of Canaan, their main objectives were to assess the strength and weaknesses of the inhabitants, the population and size of the land, and the topography and fertility of the land. This is detailed in Numbers 13:17-20.

subject: crs
text: According to the second creation story, which of the following were formed out of ground?

options.B: Beast of the field and birds

explanation: In the second creation account (Genesis 2), the sequence differs from the first. Genesis 2:19 states: "So out of the ground the Lord God formed every beast of the field and every bird of the air , and brought them to the man to see what he would call them..."

subject: crs
text: Which of the following is the reason for which God decided to make a helper fit for a man in the Garden of Eden? For
explanation: The primary reason given is that it was not good for man to be alone. Genesis 2:18 says: "Then the Lord God said, 'It is not good that the man should be alone; I will make him a helper fit for him.'" This points to companionship .

subject: crs
text: 'And the Lord said to Moses, see, I make you as God to Pharaoh; and Aaron your brother shall....' Which of the following roles was Aaron assigned? A
explanation: In Exodus 7:1, the Lord says to Moses: "See, I make you as God to Pharaoh; and Aaron your brother shall be your prophet ."

subject: crs
text: The presence of God among the Israelites is demonstrated by the symbol of
explanation: During the wilderness journey, God guided them with visible signs. Exodus 13:21: "And the Lord went before them by day in a pillar of cloud to lead them along the way, and by night in a pillar of fire to give them light..."

subject: crs
text: Joshua's success in the promised land was attributed to the fact that he was

options.B: carefree about keeping the laws of God

explanation: Success was tied to his obedience and bravery. In Joshua 1:6-7, God commands him: "Be strong and of good courage ... being careful to do according to all the law which Moses my servant commanded you."

subject: crs
text: During the mission of the twelve spies, which of the following tribes did Joshua represent?
explanation: Caleb represented Judah (Numbers 13:6). Joshua (Hoshea) represented Ephraim (Numbers 13:8: "from the tribe of Ephraim , Hoshea the son of Nun" ).
